video.title: The Sprinters Have Their Time To Shine! | Eschborn-Frankfurt 2022 Highlights

all_candidates.title: The Sprinters Have Their Time To Shine! | Eschborn-Frankfurt 2022 Highlights

transcript.clean_text: welcome to short highlights of the 59th edition of ishbourne frankfurt with me jess cox do not forget you can go back and catch the race in its entirety on gcn plus this race uh in its 64th year but the 59th edition takes on a very familiar course heading northwestward from frankfurt starting out in the city of eschborn a birthplace of tony martin no less to head northwestward up into the torness hills from very early on today a breakaway of five riders went clear and the man of the match no doubt about it was jen's reindeers because uh he was to go in two breakaways today there he was in the yellow and blue of sport london valois behind that leading group of five most of the pace setting being done by the german team of borah hansgrower and sam bennett the irishman this was the break away as they headed into the midsection of our course there at one point had in excess of five minutes of bleed but that was then pegged down to about two two and a half minutes and the team of ontamarshi wanted gobert and uh the four-time winner alexander kristoff doing an awesome lot this was gail zimmerman desperately trying to detach a group from the front end and mix things up a little bit to put pressure on the other sprinters teams the race is a fascinating mix of big wide open forest roads and then this fascinating finale we have in the center of frankfurt for the criterium finish criterium style finish i should say this was pierre hollande in the breakaway all day the remaining survivor of that first break he was then joined by some more who kicked things off and joined pierre-elante for a rolling second breakaway which again then became five riders roland being allowed a little bit of time to relax on the back of it because he was back in there once again with jen surrenders of sportlander and balwaz they were joined not too long after by jonas rich the german writer for ef education easy post and he was one of the strongest riders in there as they closed into the last 50 kilometers but the peloton was closing all the time and yen surrenders once again wound his way back on after the final climb of the mammal shine and was left out front by himself what an impressive ride by this young belgian today he was the last man to be caught having been in both breakaways today including being dropped and winding his way back on this was the final ascent of the mammal shine out in the tornes hills before a regrouping of the whole peloton and then that uh tactical game of setting up their sprinter for this big finale today this is one of the most important one day races for the sprinters on the men's world tour and alpha synthetics were doing everything they could to try and set things up for jasper phillipson last year's winner there was the bell being rung with one seven kilometer lap to go the aspar philips and last year's winner could our person fenix set things up for him bahrain victorious doing everything they could for phil bauhaus the big german sprinter and israel premier tech seeing if they could get jackie zolo into the right position alex dowsett doing a lot in the closing stages to try and string things out on the city center circuit binham germain moved up very quickly making his return the eritrean after going back home after his big win in general and this was the final lead into the final drive fred wright and south london are opening up hard binham gourmet coming over the top of him uh the eritrean and driving hard but in his wheel was alexander kristoff looking for his fifth win in this event and then danny van popple the final lead out for that man sam bennett the irishman in the end going clear of fernando gaviria and getting the win that puts him back at the top of the sprinters table the irishman believe me is formally back it's been a very testing last year or so for sam bennett and the irishman was delighted at the finish what a lead out by danny van poppel as well look at the speed of that vampo having the opportunity to sit up and celebrate as alexander kristoff cruised past him both the borah riders celebrating and sam bennett delighted gaviria sprinting well just behind him and alexander kristoff also looking mighty sprightly on the line there was the top ten bennett from gaviria and christoph phil bauhaus holding his position well in fourth just ahead of lead out man danny van popple what a comeback for sam bennett who looks very much right back to his best in this one of the two big german races final shots to close with looking down on the opel zoo in konigstein thank you for watching the highlights do catch the whole thing back on gcn plus
